dentition_metadata.clinical_note: Optimal oral health requires a synergistic approach between mechanical cleaning and pH-balanced nutrition.

individual_tooth_data.name: Upper Right 3rd Molar (Wisdom Tooth)
individual_tooth_data.description: The posterior-most tooth in the upper right quadrant. Often vestigial with variable root morphology.
individual_tooth_data.function: Grinding fibrous plant material and tough proteins.
individual_tooth_data.precautions: Extremely difficult to clean; high risk of pericoronitis (gum infection) and distal caries on adjacent Tooth #2.
individual_tooth_data.brushing_protocol: Requires a slim-headed brush or end-tuft brush. Angulate 45 degrees toward the gum line for 8-10 seconds.
individual_tooth_data.dietary_advice: Avoid sticky candies (taffy/caramel) which are impossible to clear from this depth.

individual_tooth_data.name: Upper Right 2nd Molar
individual_tooth_data.description: Large multi-cusp tooth assisting the 1st molar in heavy mastication.
individual_tooth_data.precautions: Deep occlusal pits are magnets for bacterial biofilm. Check for sealant integrity.
individual_tooth_data.brushing_protocol: Focus on the buccal (cheek) side where the parotid gland releases saliva, leading to faster tartar buildup.
individual_tooth_data.dietary_advice: High-fiber vegetables (carrots, celery) help naturally scrub the chewing surface.

individual_tooth_data.name: Upper Right 1st Molar (6-Year Molar)
individual_tooth_data.description: The largest tooth in the upper right arch; typically possesses three roots for maximum stability.
individual_tooth_data.function: Primary anchor of the bite; bears the highest force during chewing.
individual_tooth_data.precautions: Commonly the first permanent tooth to require a filling. Watch for 'vertical fracture syndrome'.
individual_tooth_data.brushing_protocol: Apply moderate pressure on the occlusal surface. Ensure the brush reaches the 'furcation' (where roots meet) if gums have recessed.
individual_tooth_data.dietary_advice: Calcium-rich dairy (cheese/yogurt) to support the massive enamel surface area.

individual_tooth_data.name: Upper Right 2nd Premolar
individual_tooth_data.description: Transition tooth with two cusps; slightly smaller than the 1st molar.
individual_tooth_data.precautions: The 'contact point' between #4 and #5 is a frequent site for 'hidden' cavities.
individual_tooth_data.brushing_protocol: Use a back-and-forth vibrating motion (Bass Technique) to clean the interproximal spaces.
individual_tooth_data.dietary_advice: Limit refined carbohydrates (crackers/breads) which stick in the grooves.

individual_tooth_data.name: Upper Right 1st Premolar
individual_tooth_data.description: Distinctive two-cusp tooth, often having a bifurcated root (two root tips).
individual_tooth_data.function: Initial crushing; supports the canine in tearing.
individual_tooth_data.precautions: The mesial (front) surface has a natural concavity that is very hard to floss properly.
individual_tooth_data.brushing_protocol: Focus on the transition from the canine to the premolar; don't skip the 'corner' of the arch.
individual_tooth_data.dietary_advice: Vitamin C-rich foods (bell peppers/broccoli) to keep the supporting periodontal ligaments strong.

individual_tooth_data.name: Upper Right Canine
individual_tooth_data.description: The longest tooth in the human mouth; the 'cornerstone' of the dental arch.
individual_tooth_data.function: Shearing food and guiding the jaw during side-to-side movement.
individual_tooth_data.precautions: Highly susceptible to 'abfraction' (notches at the gum line) due to heavy bite forces.
individual_tooth_data.brushing_protocol: Avoid aggressive horizontal scrubbing. Use soft circular motions to prevent gum recession.
individual_tooth_data.dietary_advice: Apples and firm fruits are excellent for stimulating the long root through 'functional loading'.

individual_tooth_data.name: Upper Right Lateral Incisor
individual_tooth_data.description: Smaller cutting tooth adjacent to the central incisor.
individual_tooth_data.function: Cutting food; crucial for phonetics (specifically the 'S' sound).
individual_tooth_data.precautions: Often has a 'lingual pit' on the back side where cavities form unnoticed.
individual_tooth_data.brushing_protocol: Brush the back (lingual) side vertically with the tip of the toothbrush.
individual_tooth_data.dietary_advice: Avoid biting directly into ice or hard hard candies; this tooth is prone to fracture.

individual_tooth_data.name: Upper Right Central Incisor
individual_tooth_data.description: The most visible tooth in the smile; chisel-shaped with a broad biting edge.
individual_tooth_data.function: Incising (cutting) food and supporting the upper lip for facial aesthetics.
individual_tooth_data.precautions: Most common tooth to be fractured in sports. Enamel is thin at the incisal edge.
individual_tooth_data.brushing_protocol: Gentle vertical strokes. Over-brushing here leads to 'black triangles' (loss of gum between teeth).
individual_tooth_data.dietary_advice: Minimize dark liquids (coffee/red wine) as these teeth stain the most noticeably.

individual_tooth_data.name: Upper Left Central Incisor
individual_tooth_data.description: Mirror image of #8. The focal point of dental symmetry.
individual_tooth_data.precautions: Avoid using to tear open packaging or hold metal objects (pins/needles).
individual_tooth_data.brushing_protocol: Ensure the 'sulcus' (where tooth meets gum) is cleared of plaque daily.
individual_tooth_data.dietary_advice: Drink water after consuming berries to prevent extrinsic staining.

individual_tooth_data.name: Upper Left Lateral Incisor
individual_tooth_data.description: Mirror image of #7.
individual_tooth_data.precautions: Check for 'peg lateral' (undersized) development which may require restorative care.
individual_tooth_data.brushing_protocol: Vertical brush orientation for the lingual surface.
individual_tooth_data.dietary_advice: Raw nuts provide good resistance, but chew with molars, not incisors.

individual_tooth_data.name: Upper Left Canine
individual_tooth_data.description: Mirror image of #6.
individual_tooth_data.precautions: The 'cusp tip' can wear down if the patient suffers from night grinding (bruxism).
individual_tooth_data.brushing_protocol: Use a soft-bristled brush. Spend 10 seconds specifically on the gum line.
individual_tooth_data.dietary_advice: Leafy greens provide Vitamin K, essential for bone density around the long root.

individual_tooth_data.name: Upper Left 1st Premolar
individual_tooth_data.description: Mirror image of #5.
individual_tooth_data.precautions: Root canal anatomy here is often complex; avoid deep decay at all costs.
individual_tooth_data.brushing_protocol: Sweep from the gum toward the biting edge (The Modified Stillman technique).
individual_tooth_data.dietary_advice: Lean proteins provide phosphorus, which works with calcium to protect enamel.

individual_tooth_data.name: Upper Left 2nd Premolar
individual_tooth_data.description: Mirror image of #4.
individual_tooth_data.precautions: Vulnerable to 'interproximal' decay—decay between teeth that only X-rays can see.
individual_tooth_data.brushing_protocol: Requires daily flossing to reach the 40% of the tooth a brush misses.
individual_tooth_data.dietary_advice: Avoid frequent 'grazing' on dried fruits (raisins/dates) which are high in sugar and sticky.

individual_tooth_data.name: Upper Left 1st Molar
individual_tooth_data.description: Mirror image of #3. Known as a 'cornerstone' of the arch.
individual_tooth_data.function: Heavy chewing and maintaining facial height.
individual_tooth_data.precautions: Often has a 'Cusp of Carabelli' (an extra small cusp) that can trap plaque.
individual_tooth_data.brushing_protocol: Use a power brush if possible for 10-15 seconds to ensure the deep grooves are vibrating clean.
individual_tooth_data.dietary_advice: Sugar-free gum with Xylitol after meals to stimulate saliva and neutralize acids.

individual_tooth_data.name: Upper Left 2nd Molar
individual_tooth_data.description: Mirror image of #2.
individual_tooth_data.precautions: Proximity to the sinus cavity means toothaches here can sometimes be sinus infections.
individual_tooth_data.brushing_protocol: Close your mouth slightly while brushing here to relax the cheek muscle for better access.
individual_tooth_data.dietary_advice: Green tea (unsweetened) contains polyphenols that inhibit cavity-causing bacteria.

individual_tooth_data.name: Upper Left 3rd Molar (Wisdom Tooth)
individual_tooth_data.description: Mirror image of #1.
individual_tooth_data.precautions: Check for 'partial eruption' which allows bacteria to enter under the gum flap.
individual_tooth_data.brushing_protocol: Ensure you reach the very back surface (the 'distal' face).
individual_tooth_data.dietary_advice: Avoid carbonated beverages which pool in the back of the mouth and erode the enamel.

individual_tooth_data.name: Lower Left 3rd Molar (Wisdom Tooth)
individual_tooth_data.description: Located in the lower left jaw; frequently impacted due to lack of space in the mandible.
individual_tooth_data.precautions: Highly prone to gum disease due to the 'pocket' created between it and the jawbone.
individual_tooth_data.brushing_protocol: Wiggle the brush bristles under the gum flap if the tooth is partially out.
individual_tooth_data.dietary_advice: Soft foods if the area is inflamed; avoid popcorn kernels which can get stuck in wisdom pockets.

individual_tooth_data.name: Lower Left 2nd Molar
individual_tooth_data.description: A sturdy four-cusp molar in the lower jaw.
individual_tooth_data.precautions: Gravity allows food debris to settle here more than in upper teeth.
individual_tooth_data.brushing_protocol: Focus heavily on the occlusal (top) grooves for 10 seconds.
individual_tooth_data.dietary_advice: Whole grains provide B-vitamins for healthy gum tissue around the molars.

individual_tooth_data.name: Lower Left 1st Molar
individual_tooth_data.description: One of the most important teeth for functional chewing; often the most restored tooth in adults.
individual_tooth_data.function: Primary grinding force of the lower jaw.
individual_tooth_data.precautions: Check for 'furcation' involvement (bone loss between the two roots).
individual_tooth_data.brushing_protocol: Use an electric toothbrush with a pressure sensor to avoid damaging the gums.
individual_tooth_data.dietary_advice: Limit acidic soda intake; the lower 1st molar is often 'bathed' in acid during swallowing.

individual_tooth_data.name: Lower Left 2nd Premolar
individual_tooth_data.description: A robust premolar with a more circular shape than the upper premolars.
individual_tooth_data.precautions: The mental foramen (nerve opening) is nearby; sensitivity here should be checked by a pro.
individual_tooth_data.brushing_protocol: Angle bristles 45 degrees toward the tongue-side (lingual) to remove sub-gingival plaque.
individual_tooth_data.dietary_advice: Crunchy vegetables (like peppers) help stimulate the jaw bone.

individual_tooth_data.name: Lower Left 1st Premolar
individual_tooth_data.description: A small premolar that looks somewhat like a canine from the front.
individual_tooth_data.precautions: Prone to 'abfraction' lesions (notches) at the gum line due to clenching.
individual_tooth_data.brushing_protocol: Use a 'sweep' motion from the gum line upward.
individual_tooth_data.dietary_advice: Almonds provide magnesium, which is vital for maintaining the tooth-to-bone bond.

individual_tooth_data.name: Lower Left Canine
individual_tooth_data.description: A long-rooted, powerful tooth at the corner of the lower arch.
individual_tooth_data.function: Tearing and protecting the back teeth during jaw movement.
individual_tooth_data.precautions: Watch for 'recession' on the cheek side due to the thinness of the bone over the root.
individual_tooth_data.brushing_protocol: Be very gentle on the outer surface; avoid hard-bristled brushes.
individual_tooth_data.dietary_advice: Strawberries contain malic acid, which can naturally help keep the surface clean (but rinse after!).

individual_tooth_data.name: Lower Left Lateral Incisor
individual_tooth_data.description: Narrow cutting tooth in the lower front.
individual_tooth_data.precautions: Lower incisors are the first place 'Calculus' (tartar) forms because of the salivary glands nearby.
individual_tooth_data.brushing_protocol: Spend extra time on the lingual (tongue-side) where hard deposits form.
individual_tooth_data.dietary_advice: Limit starchy snacks (chips) which turn into glue-like plaque on front teeth.

individual_tooth_data.name: Lower Left Central Incisor
individual_tooth_data.description: Smallest tooth in the human body; very narrow and chisel-shaped.
individual_tooth_data.precautions: Extremely prone to crowding, which makes cleaning between them nearly impossible without floss.
individual_tooth_data.brushing_protocol: Use 'Water-flossing' or interdental brushes if these teeth are crowded.
individual_tooth_data.dietary_advice: Drink water after every meal to flush the 'lower pool' of the mouth.

individual_tooth_data.name: Lower Right Central Incisor
individual_tooth_data.description: Mirror image of #24.
individual_tooth_data.precautions: Bone around these roots is very thin; avoid tobacco products which cause rapid bone loss here.
individual_tooth_data.brushing_protocol: Use the 'tip' of the brush for the back side; avoid a horizontal 'sawing' motion.
individual_tooth_data.dietary_advice: Vitamin D supplements or sun exposure to ensure the thin bone stays dense.

individual_tooth_data.name: Lower Right Lateral Incisor
individual_tooth_data.description: Mirror image of #23.
individual_tooth_data.precautions: Calculus buildup can lead to 'recession' and eventually tooth loss.
individual_tooth_data.brushing_protocol: Floss daily—no exceptions for lower incisors.
individual_tooth_data.dietary_advice: Onions and garlic have antimicrobial properties that help reduce plaque bacteria.

individual_tooth_data.name: Lower Right Canine
individual_tooth_data.description: Mirror image of #22.
individual_tooth_data.precautions: If you grind your teeth, the tip of this tooth will flatten first.
individual_tooth_data.brushing_protocol: Soft circular motions for 10 seconds.
individual_tooth_data.dietary_advice: Tougher textures like steak or jerky (in moderation) require the canine's tearing function.

individual_tooth_data.name: Lower Right 1st Premolar
individual_tooth_data.description: Mirror image of #21.
individual_tooth_data.precautions: Check for cervical decay (near the gum line).
individual_tooth_data.brushing_protocol: Modified Bass technique (45-degree angle).
individual_tooth_data.dietary_advice: Sugar-free yogurt provides probiotics that fight 'bad' oral bacteria.

individual_tooth_data.name: Lower Right 2nd Premolar
individual_tooth_data.description: Mirror image of #20.
individual_tooth_data.precautions: Frequent site for food impaction.
individual_tooth_data.brushing_protocol: Clean the 'distal' surface (the back) carefully.
individual_tooth_data.dietary_advice: Avoid sucking on lemons or acidic candy; the lower premolars are highly susceptible to acid wear.

individual_tooth_data.name: Lower Right 1st Molar
individual_tooth_data.description: Mirror image of #19. Usually the largest and strongest tooth in the lower arch.
individual_tooth_data.precautions: Deep grooves (fissures) on the top are almost always where the first cavity starts.
individual_tooth_data.brushing_protocol: 15 seconds of focused vibration on the chewing surface.
individual_tooth_data.dietary_advice: Foods high in Phosphorus (eggs/fish) to maintain enamel hardness.

individual_tooth_data.name: Lower Right 2nd Molar
individual_tooth_data.description: Mirror image of #18.
individual_tooth_data.precautions: Limited space between the tooth and the cheek can lead to plaque buildup.
individual_tooth_data.brushing_protocol: Use a small-head brush or an electric brush to reach the cheek-side properly.
individual_tooth_data.dietary_advice: Raw carrots are 'nature’s toothbrush' for these large grinding teeth.

individual_tooth_data.name: Lower Right 3rd Molar (Wisdom Tooth)
individual_tooth_data.description: Mirror image of #17.
individual_tooth_data.precautions: High risk of 'Pericoronitis'—inflammation of the gum flap over the tooth.
individual_tooth_data.brushing_protocol: Focus on the very back surface; gag reflex often prevents people from cleaning here—try to push through it.
individual_tooth_data.dietary_advice: Avoid seeds (chia/sesame) if you have an open socket or gum flap here, as they get trapped easily.
